title: Pooled Registered Pension Plan (PRPP) – information for individuals - Canada.ca
content:
## Pooled Registered Pension Plan (PRPP) – information for individuals
A PRPP is a retirement savings option for individuals, including self-employed individuals.
A PRPP enables its members to benefit from lower administration costs that result from participating in a large, pooled pension plan. It's also portable, so it moves with its members from job to job.
Since the investment options within a PRPP are similar to those for other registered pension plans, its members can benefit from greater flexibility in managing their savings and meeting their retirement objectives.

## Services and information
- Joining a PRPP Eligibility and participation in a PRPP
- Contributions to a PRPP Member and employer contributions, contributions on tax-exempt income
- PRPP contribution receipt slip Contribution receipt slip
- PRPP transfers Transfers to and from a PRPP
- PRPP withdrawals Withdrawals, impact on other federal benefits or credits
- PRPP life events Death of a PRPP member, breakdown of the marriage or common-law partnership
- Investment options PRPP investments
- Pooled Registered Pension Plan (PRPP) contribution receipt return
- The Pooled Registered Pension Plan (PRPP)
- Frequently asked questions
Understand your rights and obligations when dealing with the CRA.
